name: Prism Breaker
title: Brick-breaker game
summary: A fully playable neon-synthwave brick-breaker — bounce a glowing ball to smash brick walls across five levels, catching falling power-ups.
setup_questions: What should the game be called, and what's the vibe — neon synthwave, candy pastel, or retro CRT green?
What's your accent/primary color for the ball and paddle glow?
How many levels should the first run have (e.g. 5), and should difficulty ramp by speed, brick rows, or both?
Name 2–3 power-ups you'd like (or use the defaults — multi-ball, wide paddle, slow-mo).
photo_terms: abstract dark gradient with neon particles
sections: Title / start screen with PLAY, How to Play, and high-score badge
Canvas gameplay scene: paddle, ball physics, brick grid, lives, live score and level HUD
Power-up drops (multi-ball, wide paddle, slow-mo, laser) that fall and are caught
Level progression with distinct brick layouts and increasing ball speed
Game over / level-clear overlay with score, best score, and Play Again
How-to-play modal (arrow keys / mouse / touch to move, space to launch)
based_on: A neon-synthwave brick-breaker in the lineage of Atari Breakout (1976) and Taito's Arkanoid (1986) — paddle, ball, destructible brick walls, falling power-ups, multi-level progression.
